pi_items.source: task.json and export_result.sh explicitly require this path.
USGS QuakeML in data/fdsn/noto_earthquake.xml which is imported via convert_quakeml.py. The first <origin> has latitude 37.4874.

USGS QuakeML in data/fdsn/noto_earthquake.xml has depth 10000 meters.

USGS QuakeML in data/fdsn/noto_earthquake.xml has type mww.
convert_quakeml.py hardcodes agencyID to USGS during database insertion.
GEOFON StationXML in data/fdsn/ge_stations.xml which is imported to SeisComP database.

These are simply verifier configuration parameters, not empirical facts. They match the task.json exactly.

privileged_info_summary: The SeisComP database is populated with real-world earthquake and station data from USGS and GEOFON FDSN XML files. The true database coordinates for the 2024 Noto Peninsula earthquake are latitude 37.4874 and longitude 137.271, contradicting the task.json metadata's claim of 37.23 and 136.76. Furthermore, several station coordinates in the metadata are completely inaccurate compared to the true database contents; most notably, the KWP station is actually located in Poland (Lat 49.6305, Lon 22.7078) and not near Flores (Lat -8.86, Lon 121.78) as claimed. A correct agent execution will extract the true values and thus compute significantly different distances (e.g., KWP distance ~8397 km instead of ~5357 km), which the programmatic verifier will falsely flag as incorrect.
